title: God’s Voice or My Thoughts? The 30‑Second Test
hook: Before you act, run this 30‑second test: Source, Scripture, Fruit.
script: Source: Who’s speaking? Fear, ego, and hurry shout. The Shepherd leads with clarity and love. Jesus said, My sheep hear my voice. Scripture: Does it match the Bible? God won’t contradict His Word. All Scripture trains us for good, not revenge. Fruit: What grows if you obey? James says wisdom is pure, peaceable, gentle. The Spirit’s fruit looks like love, joy, peace. Quick example: You feel, Text them a harsh truth now. Scripture calls for gentleness. Fruit would be strife. Not God. Or, Apologize and own your part. That aligns with Scripture and produces peace. Breathe, pray, seek wise counsel, then move with quiet confidence.

title: Overthinking? Try the 3‑Question Discernment Check
hook: If you’re overthinking every decision, do this simple test.
script: Write the thought. Now ask: Does this sound like the Shepherd or my fear? Jesus said His sheep know His voice, not the voice of panic. Next: Does it agree with Scripture? God’s Word trains and corrects, never pushes you to sin or shame. Finally: What fruit does it produce? James says God’s wisdom is pure, peaceable, gentle, willing to yield. If it creates constant chaos, it’s likely not God. Bonus: run it by wise, godly counsel because victory comes with many advisors. Use this for texts, choices, and late‑night spirals. For the next 24 hours, test before you react and watch your anxiety drop.

title: SSF Method: Is That God or Anxiety?
hook: You need to hear this today: you can test inner nudges in under a minute.
script: Remember SSF: Source, Scripture, Fruit. Source: Is this driven by FOMO, people‑pleasing, or the calm lead of the Shepherd? Scripture: Does it line up with God’s Word? He won’t guide you into bitterness, greed, or deceit. Fruit: What shows up if you follow it—peace, purity, mercy—or confusion and pride? Example: A new job flashes big money but spikes anxiety and compromises integrity. SSF it. Seek counsel too, because wisdom grows in community. God’s voice is steady, not slippery—pure, peaceable, open to reason. My sheep hear my voice. Save this, pray it through, and let SSF turn noise into clarity this week.
